rationale: Both largely miss the reviewers' central unresolved worry (only Qwen-Image/H200, and later FLUX, were validated among ~10 listed affected model families) and neither catches the pytest-mark completeness thread. But Y engages substantively with more of the actual review history — it explicitly surfaces the FLUX.1-dev A/B justifying the global scope change, the P1+P2-vs-main perf supplement, and accuracy metrics (SSIM/PSNR) that map onto real reviewer requests, giving it much higher recall. X's findings (test-name mismatch, docstring rationale) are grounded and cleanly actionable but are novel nitpicks rather than ground-truth concerns, and it never mentions FLUX, accuracy comparisons, or the P1+P2 supplement at all. Precision and actionability are roughly comparable between the two, so recall drives the decision toward Y.
